commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=768d1ed26e218675b0b665932c43b8355d2d02a4)
commands.review_summary:
The branch defaults Slack `member-info` to the trusted same-account inbound sender and propagates `requesterAccountId` through local, Gateway, and Swift message-action surfaces.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main requires `userId` for Slack `member-info`, and the mixed-version Gateway failure follows from unchanged v4 negotiation plus a strict released `message.action` schema without `requesterAccountId`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the Gateway protocol/upgrade contract, not a narrow repair automation should choose.

target.title: fix(slack): default member-info userId to inbound sender
target.body:
## Summary

- Let Slack `member-info` omit `userId` in a current Slack conversation and default to the trusted inbound sender.
- Require the source provider and canonical Slack account to match the target; cross-channel and cross-account calls still require an explicit `userId`.
- Carry trusted requester account identity with requester sender identity through local and gateway-owned message action dispatch.

An explicit `userId` always wins.

## Root cause

The Slack tool schema already makes `userId` optional, but dispatch required it unconditionally. Defaulting only from `requesterSenderId` was not sufficient: sender IDs are provider- and workspace-scoped, so provider and account identity must travel together.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts` — 4 shards, 275 tests passed
- Azure Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` — passed; run `run_b53ae65825ba`, lease `cbx_820e5280e6d5`
- Azure Crabbox `pnpm build` — passed; run `run_1c11f81d85ff`, same lease (stopped)
- Fresh branch autoreview — no actionable findings; correctness confidence 0.94
- `git diff --check` and focused formatting — passed

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Slack `member-info` no longer fails with `userId required` when called from a same-account Slack turn without an explicit user id.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Slack action dispatch, message-tool, local plugin-dispatch, and Gateway action seams; Linux type/lint/guard/build proof on Azure Crabbox.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: 275 targeted tests passed; remote changed gate and full build passed.
- Observed result after fix: same-provider, same-canonical-account calls use the trusted inbound Slack sender; configured default and case-normalized account ids work; missing, cross-provider, and cross-account context rejects unless `userId` is explicit.
- What was not tested: a live Slack workspace call. The API invocation itself is unchanged; focused proof covers the new identity-selection and propagation behavior.

## Compatibility and risk

- Additive optional `requesterAccountId` field in the channel action context and Gateway `message.action` request.
- No config, migration, permission, token, or dependency changes.
- The account check prevents a Slack workspace-local user id from being reused with another account token.

commands.command: the code already implements owner mismatch checking at clawhub.ts:1565-1566 and executeskilluninstall blocks both skill dir and lockfile removal when ownermismatch is true (lines 1586-1592 and 1611-1613). could you clarify which specific case is still problematic?
commands.freeform_prompt: The code already implements owner mismatch checking at clawhub.ts:1565-1566 and executeSkillUninstall blocks both skill dir AND lockfile removal when ownerMismatch is true (lines 1586-1592 and 1611-1613). Could you clarify which specific case is still problematic?

target.title: feat(cli): add skills uninstall command
target.body:
## Summary

Add `openclaw skills uninstall <slug>` CLI command that cleanly removes a ClawHub-installed skill from the active workspace.

## Changes

- **`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts`**:
  - Added `SkillUninstallPlan` and `SkillUninstallResult` types
  - Added `planSkillUninstall()` and `executeSkillUninstall()`
  - **Security**: Gate directory removal on ClawHub provenance (`isClawHubInstall`)

- **`src/cli/skills-cli.ts`**:
  - Added `uninstall` command with `--dry-run`, `--yes`, `--json`, `--global`, `--agent` flags

## real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Adds a new CLI command `openclaw skills uninstall` that safely removes ClawHub-installed skills from the workspace.

**Real environment tested**: Local macOS terminal with OpenClaw CLI built from the PR branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/manual-demo
echo "# Manual" > ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/manual-demo/SKILL.md
node openclaw.mjs skills uninstall manual-demo --yes
```

**Evidence after fix**: ![screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/00f5e2cc-248f-4ea7-b8a2-07929f552901)

**Observed result after fix**:
```
Skill directory exists but is not a ClawHub install. Use "rm -rf" to remove manually.
Uninstalled manual-demo:
  ✗ removed workspace directory
  ✗ removed lockfile entry
```

The manual skill directory remains intact - NOT deleted!

**What was not tested**: Testing on Linux/Windows, testing with actual ClawHub network calls.

## Issue

Fixes #63272

---

**Labels**: clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, P2
